name: GOLFTEC Boca Raton
about: GOLFTEC is a technology-driven golf training studio that uses data and advanced motion analysis to provide personalized instruction. The company utilizes OPTIMOTION technology, high-speed video, and launch monitors to help golfers of all skill levels improve their swings. Lessons are structured around a Swing Evaluation that creates a custom game plan, with options for on-course training and club fittings. Sessions are conducted indoors in training bays equipped with launch monitors and simulators, allowing for year-round practice regardless of weather. GOLFTEC positions itself as a premium golf improvement destination, offering a clear, data-backed path to better golf for both beginners and experienced players.
why_golfers_like_it: Data-driven instruction using advanced technology like OPTIMOTION motion capture and launch monitors
Personalized improvement plans based on a comprehensive Swing Evaluation
Ability to practice and take lessons year-round in an indoor, controlled environment

faq.question: Do I need clubs?
faq.answer: Yes, you will need to bring your own golf clubs for lessons and practice sessions. GOLFTEC also offers custom club fitting services where you can be fitted for new clubs using their technology.

faq.question: Can I bring my own food and drink?
faq.answer: There is no information provided regarding bringing outside food or drink. The focus of the venue is on golf instruction and practice, not on a food and beverage service.

faq.question: How long does it take to play 18 holes?
faq.answer: Playing 18 virtual holes on a golf simulator is typically faster than a real round. A standard 18-hole simulation generally takes between 2 to 3 hours, but this can vary based on simulator software, player speed, and whether you are playing a full simulation or just using the simulator for practice.

faq.question: Is GOLFTEC kid-friendly?
faq.answer: GOLFTEC offers instruction for juniors and youth. They have a section dedicated to junior golf programs and use technology like video analysis and launch monitors to help younger golfers understand their progress. However, the primary environment is focused on individual coaching and practice, not a family entertainment center.
